instruction: Build a Next.js App Router page with server components, parallel data fetching, streaming with Suspense boundaries, and proper error handling using error.tsx.
output:
Next.js App Router fundamentally changes how we think about data fetching. Server Components fetch data directly without useEffect or client state. Key patterns: (1) Parallel data fetching - don't await sequentially, fire all fetches at once. In App Router, you can do this by having multiple async server components each fetching their own data, wrapped in Suspense boundaries. This enables streaming - the page shell renders immediately, and each section streams in as its data resolves. (2) Suspense boundaries control the loading UI granularity. Without them, the entire page waits for all data. With them, each section shows its own skeleton. (3) Error handling uses the error.tsx convention - a client component that catches errors from its route segment. It receives error and reset props. The reset function retries the server component render. (4) Loading.tsx provides segment-level loading UI that wraps the page in a Suspense boundary automatically. The key insight: server components can be async functions that await directly, and React's streaming SSR sends HTML chunks as each component resolves. ```typescript // app/dashboard/page.tsx - Main page with parallel data fetching import { Suspense } from 'react'; import { UserProfile } from './components/user-profile'; import { RecentActivity } from './components/recent-activity'; import { Analytics } from './components/analytics'; import { Notifications } from './components/notifications'; export default function DashboardPage() { // No data fetching here - delegate to child server components // Each component fetches independently, enabling streaming return (
}>
}>
}>
}>
); } function ProfileSkeleton() { return
; } function AnalyticsSkeleton() { return
; } function ActivitySkeleton() { return
; } function NotificationsSkeleton() { return
; } // app/dashboard/components/analytics.tsx - Server component with direct fetch async function fetchAnalytics() { const res = await fetch('https://api.example.com/analytics', { next: { revalidate: 60 }, // ISR: revalidate every 60s }); if (!res.ok) throw new Error(`Analytics fetch failed: ${res.status}`); return res.json() as Promise<{ pageViews: number; visitors: number; bounceRate: number }>; } export async function Analytics() { const data = await fetchAnalytics(); return (

Analytics

); } function Stat({ label, value }: { label: string; value: string }) { return (
{value}
{label}
); } // app/dashboard/error.tsx - Error boundary (must be client component) 'use client'; export default function DashboardError({ error, reset, }: { error: Error & { digest?: string }; reset: () => void; }) { return (

Dashboard Error

{error.message}

{error.digest && (

Error ID: {error.digest}

)}
); } ```

instruction: Implement Next.js Server Actions for form handling with validation, optimistic updates, error handling, and progressive enhancement that works without JavaScript.
output:
Server Actions in Next.js are a powerful pattern for mutations. They run on the server but can be called from client components via form actions or direct invocation. Key considerations: (1) Progressive enhancement - forms with action={serverAction} work without JS because they submit as regular HTML forms. The server processes the FormData and can redirect or revalidate. (2) Validation should happen both client-side (UX) and server-side (security). Use zod for schema validation on the server. (3) Optimistic updates with useOptimistic hook - show the expected result immediately while the server action runs. If it fails, revert. (4) Error handling - server actions can return error objects or throw. Using useActionState (React 19) / useFormState gives us a way to return validation errors back to the form. The pattern: define 'use server' action, validate with zod, perform mutation, call revalidatePath or redirect. For the client, useActionState wraps the action to get pending state and return value. The form works progressively - without JS it posts and redirects, with JS it's enhanced with optimistic UI. ```typescript // app/actions/todo-actions.ts 'use server'; import { revalidatePath } from 'next/cache'; import { redirect } from 'next/navigation'; import { z } from 'zod'; const TodoSchema = z.object({ title: z.string().min(1, 'Title is required').max(200, 'Title too long'), priority: z.enum(['low', 'medium', 'high']), dueDate: z.string().optional().refine( (d) => !d || !isNaN(Date.parse(d)), 'Invalid date format' ), }); export type ActionState = { errors?: Record; message?: string; success?: boolean; }; export async function createTodo( prevState: ActionState, formData: FormData ): Promise { // Server-side validation const raw = { title: formData.get('title'), priority: formData.get('priority'), dueDate: formData.get('dueDate'), }; const validated = TodoSchema.safeParse(raw); if (!validated.success) { return { errors: validated.error.flatten().fieldErrors, message: 'Validation failed', success: false, }; } try { // Perform mutation await db.todo.create({ data: { title: validated.data.title, priority: validated.data.priority, dueDate: validated.data.dueDate ? new Date(validated.data.dueDate) : null, }, }); } catch (error) { return { message: 'Failed to create todo. Please try again.', success: false, }; } revalidatePath('/todos'); return { message: 'Todo created!', success: true }; } export async function deleteTodo(id: string): Promise { try { await db.todo.delete({ where: { id } }); revalidatePath('/todos'); return { success: true }; } catch { return { message: 'Failed to delete', success: false }; } } export async function toggleTodo(id: string, completed: boolean): Promise { await db.todo.update({ where: { id }, data: { completed } }); revalidatePath('/todos'); } // Simulated db for example const db = { todo: { create: async (args: any) => ({ id: crypto.randomUUID(), ...args.data }), delete: async (args: any) => {}, update: async (args: any) => {}, }, }; // app/todos/components/todo-form.tsx 'use client'; import { useActionState, useOptimistic, useRef } from 'react'; import { createTodo, type ActionState } from '@/app/actions/todo-actions'; const initialState: ActionState = {}; export function TodoForm({ onOptimisticAdd }: { onOptimisticAdd?: (title: string) => void; }) { const [state, formAction, isPending] = useActionState(createTodo, initialState); const formRef = useRef(null); return (
{ // Optimistic update const title = formData.get('title') as string; if (title && onOptimisticAdd) onOptimisticAdd(title); await formAction(formData); if (!state.errors) formRef.current?.reset(); }} className="space-y-4 bg-white p-6 rounded-lg shadow" >
{state.errors?.title && (

{state.errors.title[0]}

)}
{state.message && (

{state.message}

)}
); } ```

instruction: Create Next.js middleware for authentication, geo-based routing, A/B testing, and request logging that composes cleanly and handles edge runtime constraints.
output:
<think>
Next.js middleware runs at the edge (Edge Runtime), which means no Node.js APIs - no fs, no native modules, limited to Web APIs. Middleware executes before every matched route and can: rewrite URLs, redirect, set headers, return responses. Key patterns: (1) Authentication - check for session cookie/JWT, redirect to login if missing, skip for public paths. (2) Geo-routing - use request.geo (provided by Vercel) or headers for region-based content. (3) A/B testing - assign users to cohorts via cookies, rewrite to variant pages. (4) Request logging - structured logging with timing. Composition is the challenge - Next.js only allows one middleware.ts file. We need a composition pattern where multiple middleware functions run in sequence, each optionally short-circuiting with a response. A chain-of-responsibility pattern works well: each middleware gets the request and a next() function. If it returns a response, the chain stops. We also need to be careful about matcher config - middleware shouldn't run on static assets, _next paths, or favicon.
</think>

```typescript
// middleware.ts
import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from 'next/server';

type MiddlewareFn = (
  request: NextRequest,
  response: NextResponse
) => Promise<NextResponse | null> | NextResponse | null;

// Middleware composer - runs each fn in order, stops on redirect
async function composeMiddleware(
  request: NextRequest,
  middlewares: MiddlewareFn[]
): Promise<NextResponse> {
  let response = NextResponse.next();

  for (const mw of middlewares) {
    const result = await mw(request, response);
    if (result) {
      // Check if it's a redirect (not just header additions)
      if (result.headers.get('location') || result.status >= 300) {
        return result;
      }
      response = result;
    }
  }
  return response;
}

// --- Auth Middleware ---
const PUBLIC_PATHS = new Set(['/', '/login', '/signup', '/api/auth', '/about']);

function authMiddleware(request: NextRequest, response: NextResponse): NextResponse | null {
  const { pathname } = request.nextUrl;

  // Skip public paths
  if (PUBLIC_PATHS.has(pathname) || pathname.startsWith('/api/public')) {
    return null;
  }

  const sessionToken = request.cookies.get('session')?.value;
  if (!sessionToken) {
    const loginUrl = new URL('/login', request.url);
    loginUrl.searchParams.set('redirect', pathname);
    return NextResponse.redirect(loginUrl);
  }

  // Validate JWT structure (full validation happens server-side)
  try {
    const [, payload] = sessionToken.split('.');
    const decoded = JSON.parse(atob(payload));
    if (decoded.exp && decoded.exp * 1000 < Date.now()) {
      return NextResponse.redirect(new URL('/login?expired=true', request.url));
    }
    // Propagate user info via headers for downstream use
    response.headers.set('x-user-id', decoded.sub || '');
    response.headers.set('x-user-role', decoded.role || 'user');
  } catch {
    return NextResponse.redirect(new URL('/login', request.url));
  }

  return response;
}

// --- Geo Routing Middleware ---
function geoMiddleware(request: NextRequest, response: NextResponse): NextResponse | null {
  const country = request.geo?.country || request.headers.get('x-vercel-ip-country') || 'US';
  const city = request.geo?.city || '';

  response.headers.set('x-user-country', country);
  response.headers.set('x-user-city', city);

  // Redirect EU users to EU-compliant pages if needed
  const euCountries = new Set(['DE', 'FR', 'IT', 'ES', 'NL', 'BE', 'PL', 'SE']);
  if (euCountries.has(country) && request.nextUrl.pathname === '/pricing') {
    const url = request.nextUrl.clone();
    url.pathname = '/pricing/eu';
    return NextResponse.rewrite(url);
  }

  return response;
}

// --- A/B Testing Middleware ---
function abTestMiddleware(request: NextRequest, response: NextResponse): NextResponse | null {
  const pathname = request.nextUrl.pathname;

  // Only apply to specific pages
  if (pathname !== '/landing') return null;

  // Check existing cohort assignment
  let cohort = request.cookies.get('ab-cohort')?.value;

  if (!cohort) {
    // Assign to cohort (50/50 split)
    cohort = Math.random() < 0.5 ? 'control' : 'variant-a';
    response.cookies.set('ab-cohort', cohort, {
      httpOnly: true,
      maxAge: 60 * 60 * 24 * 30, // 30 days
      sameSite: 'lax',
    });
  }

  // Rewrite to variant page (same URL, different content)
  if (cohort === 'variant-a') {
    const url = request.nextUrl.clone();
    url.pathname = '/landing/variant-a';
    const rewritten = NextResponse.rewrite(url);
    // Copy over cookies and headers from response
    response.cookies.getAll().forEach((c) => rewritten.cookies.set(c));
    return rewritten;
  }

  response.headers.set('x-ab-cohort', cohort);
  return response;
}

// --- Logging Middleware ---
function loggingMiddleware(request: NextRequest, response: NextResponse): NextResponse | null {
  const requestId = crypto.randomUUID();
  response.headers.set('x-request-id', requestId);

  // Edge logging (structured for log aggregation)
  console.log(JSON.stringify({
    type: 'request',
    request_id: requestId,
    method: request.method,
    path: request.nextUrl.pathname,
    country: request.geo?.country,
    user_agent: request.headers.get('user-agent')?.substring(0, 100),
    timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
  }));

  return response;
}

// --- Main middleware export ---
export async function middleware(request: NextRequest) {
  return composeMiddleware(request, [
    loggingMiddleware,
    authMiddleware,
    geoMiddleware,
    abTestMiddleware,
  ]);
}

export const config = {
  matcher: [
    // Match all paths except static files and Next internals
    '/((?!_next/static|_next/image|favicon.ico|.*\\.(?:svg|png|jpg|jpeg|gif|webp)$).*)',
  ],
};
```

instruction: Build a Next.js data fetching layer with ISR (Incremental Static Regeneration), on-demand revalidation via webhook, cache tags, and fallback strategies for stale data.
output:
Next.js App Router's data fetching with ISR has evolved significantly. The key mechanisms: (1) Time-based revalidation via fetch option `next: { revalidate: N }` - the page is statically generated and served from cache, then regenerated in the background after N seconds. Stale-while-revalidate pattern. (2) On-demand revalidation via `revalidatePath()` or `revalidateTag()` in Route Handlers - when your CMS publishes content, it calls your webhook which triggers revalidation. (3) Cache tags allow fine-grained invalidation - tag a fetch with `next: { tags: ['posts'] }` and revalidate all fetches with that tag at once. (4) Fallback strategies: when generating a page that wasn't pre-rendered, you can show a loading state (loading.tsx) or use generateStaticParams for known paths. For the data layer, we want a centralized fetching module that handles: typed responses, error boundaries, cache configuration per entity type, and stale data fallback (if the revalidation fetch fails, keep serving the old page). The fetch cache integrates with Next.js Data Cache which persists across deployments. ```typescript // lib/data/fetcher.ts - Centralized data fetching with ISR config import { unstable_cache } from 'next/cache'; type CacheConfig = { revalidate?: number; // seconds tags?: string[]; }; const API_BASE = process.env.API_URL || 'https://api.example.com'; class DataFetchError extends Error { constructor( message: string, public status: number, public url: string ) { super(message); this.name = 'DataFetchError'; } } async function fetchAPI( path: string, config: CacheConfig = {}, init?: RequestInit ): Promise { const url = `${API_BASE}${path}`; const { revalidate = 60, tags = [] } = config; const res = await fetch(url, { ...init, next: { revalidate, tags, }, headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', Authorization: `Bearer ${process.env.API_TOKEN}`, ...init?.headers, }, }); if (!res.ok) { throw new DataFetchError( `API error: ${res.statusText}`, res.status, url ); } return res.json() as Promise; } // --- Entity-specific fetchers with cache tags --- export type Post = { id: string; title: string; content: string; slug: string; author: { name: string; avatar: string }; publishedAt: string; }; export type Product = { id: string; name: string; price: number; description: string; inStock: boolean; }; export const posts = { list: (page = 1) => fetchAPI<{ data: Post[]; total: number }>( `/posts?page=${page}&limit=20`, { revalidate: 120, tags: ['posts', 'posts-list'] } ), bySlug: (slug: string) => fetchAPI( `/posts/${slug}`, { revalidate: 300, tags: ['posts', `post-${slug}`] } ), // For generateStaticParams - longer cache, fetched at build time allSlugs: () => fetchAPI( '/posts/slugs', { revalidate: 3600, tags: ['posts-slugs'] } ), }; export const products = { list: (category?: string) => fetchAPI( `/products${category ? `?category=${category}` : ''}`, { revalidate: 60, tags: ['products', `products-${category || 'all'}`] } ), byId: (id: string) => fetchAPI( `/products/${id}`, { revalidate: 30, tags: ['products', `product-${id}`] } // Shorter for stock ), }; // --- On-demand revalidation webhook --- // app/api/revalidate/route.ts import { revalidateTag, revalidatePath } from 'next/cache'; import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from 'next/server'; const WEBHOOK_SECRET = process.env.REVALIDATION_SECRET; export async function POST(request: NextRequest) { const authHeader = request.headers.get('authorization'); if (authHeader !== `Bearer ${WEBHOOK_SECRET}`) { return NextResponse.json({ error: 'Unauthorized' }, { status: 401 }); } const body = await request.json() as { type: 'post' | 'product' | 'all'; slug?: string; id?: string; }; const revalidated: string[] = []; switch (body.type) { case 'post': revalidateTag('posts'); revalidated.push('posts'); if (body.slug) { revalidateTag(`post-${body.slug}`); revalidatePath(`/blog/${body.slug}`); revalidated.push(`post-${body.slug}`); } break; case 'product': revalidateTag('products'); revalidated.push('products'); if (body.id) { revalidateTag(`product-${body.id}`); revalidated.push(`product-${body.id}`); } break; case 'all': revalidateTag('posts'); revalidateTag('products'); revalidated.push('posts', 'products'); break; } return NextResponse.json({ revalidated, timestamp: Date.now(), }); } // --- Stale data fallback with unstable_cache --- // For critical data that must always render something export const getCachedProduct = unstable_cache( async (id: string): Promise => { try { return await products.byId(id); } catch (error) { console.error(`Failed to fetch product ${id}, serving stale:`, error); return null; // Component should handle null with last-known-good UI } }, ['product-cache'], { revalidate: 30, tags: ['products'] } ); // --- Usage in page component --- // app/blog/[slug]/page.tsx import { posts } from '@/lib/data/fetcher'; import { notFound } from 'next/navigation'; export async function generateStaticParams() { const slugs = await posts.allSlugs(); return slugs.map((slug) => ({ slug })); } export default async function BlogPost({ params }: { params: { slug: string } }) { try { const post = await posts.bySlug(params.slug); return (

{post.title}

{new Date(post.publishedAt).toLocaleDateString()}
); } catch (error) { if (error instanceof DataFetchError && error.status === 404) { notFound(); } throw error; // Let error.tsx handle it } } ```
